statement.markdown: Place N queens on an NxN chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. A queen can only attack a queen of opposite color if they are in the same row, column, or diagonal. The task is to determine if such a configuration is possible with a given number of queens.
statement.inputFormat: Input consists of a line with an integer N (1 <= N <= 10000), which is the size of the chessboard.
statement.outputFormat: Output 'YES' if a valid arrangement is possible, 'NO' otherwise.
statement.notes: A queen can only be placed in a cell if no queen of opposite color is attacking it.

hints.text: Use backtracking to ensure valid queen placements.

editorial.title: Valid N-Queens Configurations using Backtracking
editorial.intuition: Start with an empty board and place queens one by one, ensuring each placement is valid.
editorial.bruteForce: Use brute force to try all possible queen positions.
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement a backtracking algorithm to efficiently find all valid queen placements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Ensure that each placed queen does not attack any previously placed queen.
editorial.dryRun: Try placing a queen at row 0, column 1 on the board and see if it can be placed.
commonMistakes: Placing queens in the same column
Placing queens in the same row
